items.text:
Hi everyone,

Our Photography Club meeting has been moved to this Thursday at 6:30 PM in Room 118, Carver Hall. Please bring the $5 print fee so we can order your gallery photos. See you there!

Best,
Maya Lopez

questions.stem: According to the email, what should members bring to the meeting?

options.A: A five-dollar printing payment
options.B: A printed photo gallery
options.D: A signed permission form

questions.explanation: The email asks members to bring the $5 print fee so the club can order gallery photos. 'A five-dollar printing payment' paraphrases this fee.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the original meeting plan?

options.A: It was going to be held outdoors.
options.B: It had been canceled completely.
options.D: It was set for a different time.

questions.explanation: Because the meeting 'has been moved to this Thursday at 6:30 PM,' the original plan must have been scheduled for a different day or time.

items.text:
Dear Ms. Okafor,

The interlibrary loan book you requested has arrived. Please pick it up at the Hutchins Library front desk by Friday, May 22. Loans are held for seven days only, so stop by soon.

Thank you,
Circulation Services

questions.stem: According to the email, where should Ms. Okafor collect the book?

options.A: At the library's main service counter
options.B: At the circulation manager's office
options.D: At the interlibrary loan center

questions.explanation: The email directs her to the Hutchins Library front desk, which 'the library's main service counter' paraphrases.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the loaned book?

options.A: It must be renewed every week.
options.B: It was purchased for the library.
options.C: It will be returned if not collected.
options.D: It was damaged during shipping.

questions.explanation: Since loans are held for only seven days, an uncollected book would not stay indefinitely and would be sent back to its lending library.

items.text:
Hi Daniel,

A package addressed to you arrived at the Brunson Hall mailroom today. It's available for pickup between 3:00 and 7:00 PM on weekdays. Remember to bring your student ID to claim it.

Thanks,
Resident Assistant Priya

questions.stem: According to the email, what must Daniel bring to collect the package?

options.A: A printed pickup slip
options.B: A package tracking number
options.D: A valid student ID card

questions.explanation: The email tells Daniel to bring his student ID to claim the package, matching 'a valid student ID card.'

questions.stem: In the email, the word "claim" is closest in meaning to

options.A: describe
options.B: collect
options.D: deliver

questions.explanation: Here 'claim it' means to take possession of the package, so 'collect' is the closest meaning.

items.text: NOTICE: The recycling bins outside Dalton Hall will be relocated to South Lot C on June 10. During the move, a temporary $2 bag fee applies at the campus store. Compost containers remain in their current location near the loading dock.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what will happen on June 10?

options.A: The campus store will close early.
options.B: The recycling bins will be moved.
options.D: The loading dock will be repaved.

questions.explanation: The notice states the recycling bins outside Dalton Hall will be relocated to South Lot C on June 10.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the compost containers?

options.A: They will be removed permanently.
options.B: They will require a usage fee.
options.C: They will stay where they are.
options.D: They will replace the recycling bins.

questions.explanation: The notice says compost containers remain in their current location, so unlike the recycling bins, they will not be moved.

items.text: ATTENTION RESIDENTS: Laundry machines in Keller Hall will be serviced on Tuesday, April 8, from 8:00 AM to noon. During this period, residents may use the free machines in Adler Hall. Normal $1.50 wash cycles resume Wednesday morning.

questions.stem: According to the notice, where can residents do laundry during the service period?

options.A: At the Keller Hall basement units
options.B: At the Adler Hall machines
options.D: At the residence front desk

questions.explanation: The notice states that during servicing, residents may use the free machines in Adler Hall.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about laundry costs on Wednesday?

options.A: Machines will charge the usual rate.
options.B: Machines will remain free of charge.
options.C: Machines will cost double the price.
options.D: Machines will accept cards only.

questions.explanation: Since normal $1.50 wash cycles resume Wednesday morning, the usual fee will once again apply.

items.text:
Writing Center Spring Hours

Monday-Thursday: 10:00 AM - 8:00 PM
Friday: 10:00 AM - 4:00 PM
Location: Sutton Hall, Room 203

Drop-in sessions are free. Appointments cost $3 and can be booked online with Dr. Reyes through Sunday, May 4.

questions.stem: According to the schedule, how much does a booked appointment cost?

options.A: A three-dollar charge
options.B: A five-dollar charge
options.D: A ten-dollar charge

questions.explanation: The schedule states that appointments cost $3, which matches 'a three-dollar charge.'

questions.stem: According to the schedule, when does the Writing Center close on Fridays?

options.A: At eight in the evening
options.B: At ten in the morning
options.D: At noon on weekdays

questions.explanation: The Friday hours listed are 10:00 AM to 4:00 PM, so the center closes at four in the afternoon.

items.text:
USED TEXTBOOK SALE!

This Saturday, May 17, 9 AM-2 PM in the Student Union lobby. Hundreds of titles from $4. Bring your old books to trade for store credit. Cash and campus cards accepted. Hosted by the Economics Society.

questions.stem: According to the advertisement, what can students do with their old books?

options.A: Exchange them for store credit
options.B: Donate them to the library
options.D: Recycle them at the union

questions.explanation: The ad invites students to bring old books to trade for store credit, matching 'exchange them for store credit.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about payment at the sale?

options.A: Only exact change is permitted.
options.B: More than one payment method works.
options.D: Checks are the preferred option.

questions.explanation: Because both cash and campus cards are accepted, buyers can pay using more than one method.

items.text: POSTED: Shuttle service to the downtown campus will be suspended on Friday, March 6, for road repairs. A replacement van departs from Lewis Plaza every thirty minutes starting at 7:30 AM. A $1 fare is required in exact change.

questions.stem: According to the notice, why is the shuttle service being suspended?

options.A: Because of a driver shortage
options.B: Because of scheduled road work
options.D: Because of low ridership

questions.explanation: The notice states the shuttle will be suspended on March 6 for road repairs, which 'scheduled road work' paraphrases.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about riders of the replacement van?

options.A: They should carry exact coins.
options.B: They must reserve a seat first.
options.C: They can ride without any fare.
options.D: They need a printed shuttle pass.

questions.explanation: Since a $1 fare is required in exact change, riders should bring exact coins to pay.

items.text:
Notice: Anderson Hall Study Room Reservation Policy

Effective September 12, the Library Services Department will require all students to reserve group study rooms in advance. Walk-in access will no longer be permitted during peak hours.

Reservation Guidelines:
- Rooms may be booked through the student portal at library.campus.edu/rooms
- Each student may reserve one room for up to two hours per day
- Reservations open seven days before the desired date
- A valid student ID must be presented upon arrival

Please note the following policies:
- Students who arrive more than 15 minutes late will forfeit their reservation
- A $5 late-cancellation fee applies if a booking is not cancelled at least four hours in advance
- Rooms must be vacated promptly at the end of each session

Group study rooms are located on the second and third floors of Anderson Hall. Standard hours are 8:00 AM to 10:00 PM, Monday through Friday, and 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM on weekends.

For assistance, please contact the Library Help Desk at helpdesk@campus.edu or visit Room 110 during regular hours.

format_metadata.title: Anderson Hall Study Room Reservation Policy

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this notice?

options.A: To announce a new system for booking study spaces
options.B: To advertise newly renovated rooms in Anderson Hall
options.C: To remind students about overdue library materials
options.D: To explain changes to the library's weekend hours

questions.explanation: The notice synthesizes its overall message across the opening and the guidelines: it introduces a requirement that students reserve group study rooms in advance through the portal. Option B introduces renovation (not mentioned); C introduces overdue materials (a plausible library topic but absent); D names weekend hours, which appear only as one supporting detail (too narrow).

questions.stem: According to the notice, what happens if a student arrives 20 minutes after their session begins?

options.A: They are charged a five-dollar penalty fee
options.B: They lose their reserved room for that session
options.C: They receive a warning through the student portal
options.D: They must rebook the room for a later time

questions.explanation: The notice states students who arrive more than 15 minutes late will forfeit their reservation; 20 minutes exceeds that threshold, so the reservation is lost (synonym substitution: forfeit to lose). Option A swaps in the $5 fee, which applies to late cancellation, not late arrival (uses_passage_words, wrong relationship). C introduces a portal warning (new term). D introduces rebooking (plausible_generic, not stated).

questions.stem: What can be inferred about students who wish to use a study room next Saturday?

options.A: They are limited to a single weekday booking
options.B: They will be charged an additional weekend fee
options.C: They can reserve the room beginning the prior Saturday
options.D: They are required to arrive before the room opens

questions.explanation: The notice states reservations open seven days before the desired date, so a Saturday booking can be made starting the previous Saturday (synthesizes the reservation window with the day). Option A reverses the rule by implying weekday-only booking (opposite logic). B introduces a weekend fee (new term, not supported). D distorts the late-arrival rule into an absolute arrival-before-opening requirement (uses_passage_words, wrong relationship).

items.text:
Subject: Volunteer Sign-Up for the Spring Sustainability Fair

Dear Students,

The Office of Campus Life is recruiting volunteers for the Spring Sustainability Fair, which will take place on April 18 from 9:00 AM to 3:00 PM on the Hartman Quad. We're looking for enthusiastic students to help make this year's event our biggest yet.

Volunteers are needed for the following roles:
- Registration desk (greeting guests and distributing maps)
- Booth assistance (supporting vendors and exhibitors)
- Clean-up crew (post-event recycling and sorting)

Each volunteer will receive a free event T-shirt and a $10 voucher for the food trucks on the quad. Those who complete a full shift will also gain early access to next semester's workshop registration.

To sign up, please complete the online form by April 4. Volunteers must attend a brief orientation on April 11 in Mercer Hall, Room 215. If you have questions, feel free to email us at campuslife@campus.edu. We hope you'll join us!

Best regards,
The Office of Campus Life

format_metadata.subject: Volunteer Sign-Up for the Spring Sustainability Fair

questions.stem: According to the email, what will every volunteer receive?

options.A: A printed map of the event venue
options.B: A free shirt and a food voucher
options.D: A reserved spot at the vendor booths

questions.explanation: The email states each volunteer will receive a free event T-shirt and a $10 voucher for the food trucks (synonym substitution: shirt for T-shirt, food voucher for the food-truck voucher). Option A borrows maps, which volunteers distribute to guests rather than receive (uses_passage_words, wrong relationship). C distorts the early workshop access into a discount (plausible_generic, not stated). D introduces a reserved booth spot (new term).

questions.stem: What can be inferred about a volunteer who works a complete shift?

options.A: They must pay a deposit for the event T-shirt
options.B: They are required to attend two separate orientations
options.C: They can register for workshops before other students
options.D: They will be assigned to the clean-up crew only

questions.explanation: The email says those who complete a full shift gain early access to next semester's workshop registration, implying they can register before students who did not volunteer (synthesizes the shift condition with the early-access benefit). Option A introduces a deposit and reverses the T-shirt being free (opposite logic). B exaggerates the single orientation into two (uses_passage_words, wrong number). D narrows roles to clean-up only, an unsupported absolute (plausible_generic).

questions.stem: According to the email, what must students do before April 11?

options.A: To report to the Hartman Quad
options.B: To collect a voucher from the office
options.C: To submit the online sign-up form
options.D: To select a preferred volunteer shift

questions.explanation: The email instructs students to complete the online form by April 4, which falls before the April 11 orientation, so submitting the form is the required prior step (synthesizes two dates with the sign-up instruction). Option A borrows Hartman Quad, the event site, not a pre-April-11 task (entity swap). B introduces collecting a voucher in advance (date/time distortion, vouchers are given at the event). D introduces shift selection (new term, not described). Note: all options use parallel 'To + verb' phrasing.

items.text:
Notice: Temporary Closure of the Bennett Science Library

The Bennett Science Library will be closed for facility maintenance from Monday, October 6, through Friday, October 10. During this period, all study spaces, computer stations, and printing services in the building will be unavailable.

Students are encouraged to use the following alternatives:
- The Main Library on Forest Avenue (open 7:00 AM to midnight daily)
- The 24-hour study lounge in Carver Hall, Room 102
- Online databases, accessible anytime through the library portal

Important reminders:
- Materials due during the closure week will be automatically extended to October 13
- Items currently on hold may be picked up at the Main Library circulation desk
- A $0.25 daily fine still applies to items not returned by the extended deadline

The library's book-return drop box near the south entrance will remain open throughout the week for your convenience.

The Bennett Science Library will reopen on Monday, October 13, at 8:00 AM. We apologize for any inconvenience and thank you for your patience. Questions may be directed to the circulation desk at bennett@campus.edu.

format_metadata.title: Temporary Closure of the Bennett Science Library

questions.stem: According to the notice, what happens to materials that are due during the closure week?

options.A: They incur a daily fine immediately
options.B: Their due dates are extended to October 13
options.C: They must be returned to Carver Hall
options.D: Their holds are cancelled until reopening

questions.explanation: The notice states materials due during the closure week will be automatically extended to October 13 (near-verbatim but restructured into a passive description). Option A reverses the timing, since the $0.25 fine applies only after the extended deadline (uses_passage_words, wrong relationship). C borrows Carver Hall, the study lounge, not a return location (entity swap). D introduces hold cancellation and contradicts the notice, which says holds may be picked up (opposite logic).

questions.stem: What can be inferred about a student who needs to print a document on October 8?

options.A: They must wait until the library reopens
options.B: They will be charged a printing surcharge
options.C: They can use the Main Library on Forest Avenue
options.D: They should email the circulation desk first

questions.explanation: October 8 falls within the closure week when printing in Bennett is unavailable, and the notice lists the Main Library as an alternative, so the student can print there (synthesizes the closure dates with the listed alternative). Option A ignores the alternatives and reverses the notice's guidance (opposite logic). B introduces a surcharge (new term). D distorts the contact line into a prerequisite for printing (uses_passage_words, wrong relationship).

questions.stem: According to the notice, which service remains available at Bennett during the closure?

options.A: The book-return drop box stays open
options.B: The computer stations stay operational
options.D: The study spaces stay reservable

questions.explanation: The notice says the book-return drop box near the south entrance will remain open throughout the week (synonym substitution: stays open for will remain open). Options B, C, and D each name services the notice explicitly lists as unavailable during the closure (study spaces, computer stations, and printing services), making them plausible because they borrow real passage terms but contradict the closure statement (uses_passage_words, wrong relationship). All options use parallel 'The + noun + stays' phrasing.

items.text:
Notice: Fall Career Networking Night

The Career Development Center invites all juniors and seniors to attend the Fall Career Networking Night on Thursday, November 13, from 5:30 PM to 8:00 PM in the Whitfield Center ballroom. This event connects students with recruiters from more than forty regional companies across technology, finance, healthcare, and nonprofit sectors.

What to bring and how to prepare:
- At least ten printed copies of your current resume
- Professional business attire is strongly recommended
- A list of target companies, available on the event portal

Registration details:
- Pre-registration is required by November 10 through the Career Center portal
- A $3 materials fee covers your name badge and a printed company directory
- On-site registration will not be available

Light refreshments will be served throughout the evening. A professional photographer will also offer complimentary headshots near the main entrance from 5:30 PM to 7:00 PM.

Students who attend will be entered into a drawing for a free professional resume review. For questions, please contact the Career Development Center at careers@campus.edu or stop by Mercer Hall, Room 300.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what must students do by November 10?

options.A: To bring ten resume copies
options.B: To register through the portal
options.D: To select a target company

questions.explanation: The notice states pre-registration is required by November 10 through the Career Center portal (synonym substitution: register for pre-registration). Option A borrows the ten resume copies, which are brought to the event rather than submitted by the deadline (uses_passage_words, wrong relationship). C distorts the $3 materials fee into a refreshments charge, though refreshments are described as served, not paid for (uses_passage_words, wrong relationship). D borrows target company from the preparation list (entity swap). All options use parallel 'To + verb' phrasing.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this notice?

options.A: To inform students about a recruiting event and how to register
options.B: To describe recent hiring trends in the technology sector
options.C: To announce the opening of a new campus career center
options.D: To remind seniors about an upcoming resume-writing class

questions.explanation: The notice synthesizes its purpose across multiple sections: it announces the Career Networking Night with recruiters and explains the required registration and preparation steps. Option B introduces hiring trends (a plausible career topic but not the focus). C introduces a new center opening (new term). D narrows the message to the resume review drawing, only one supporting detail (too narrow).

questions.stem: What can be inferred about a student who arrives at 7:30 PM without having registered?

options.A: They can complete registration at the entrance desk
options.B: They will still receive a printed company directory
options.C: They have missed the chance to obtain a free headshot
options.D: They are automatically entered into the resume drawing

questions.explanation: The notice says complimentary headshots are offered from 5:30 PM to 7:00 PM, so a student arriving at 7:30 PM has missed that window (synthesizes the arrival time with the headshot schedule). Option A contradicts the statement that on-site registration will not be available (opposite logic). B assumes the directory despite the unregistered status, which the $3 fee covers for registrants (uses_passage_words, wrong relationship). D overlooks that the drawing is for students who attend as registered participants (plausible_generic, date/time distortion).
